description: Detection specs for PII commonly found in Windows servicing/diagnostic logs (CBS, DISM, SxS). Portable, language-agnostic regex specs: each detector's full match IS the sensitive token (context is excluded with look-around), so a redactor can replace match[0] directly. `safe` describes what the token looks like AFTER redaction — a redactor skips tokens that already match it (idempotent), and a residual-PII scanner treats them as clean (so it flags only un-redacted, real-looking PII).
notes: IPv4 is deliberately ABSENT: in CBS/DISM the dotted-quad shape is overwhelmingly a Windows version (10.0.0.0, 6.4.1.0), so redacting it would corrupt version strings and break AutoExtract patterns for ~zero PII gain. Use the `generic` profile if you truly need IP redaction.
Only USER SIDs (S-1-5-21-...) are matched; well-known/system SIDs (S-1-5-18/19/20, S-1-5-32-*) are not PII and are left intact.

Microsoft WinSxS assembly names (amd64_microsoft-windows-*_31bf3856ad364e35_...) are NOT redacted: they are public component identifiers and are load-bearing for corruption analysis. A separate opt-in detector can target third-party assembly names when needed.

detectors.name: win-username-path
detectors.description: Local account name in a \Users\<name> profile path (the most common PII in CBS/DISM).

detectors.name: user-sid
detectors.description: User/domain security identifier. Machine+RID identify an account; the well-known prefix S-1-5-21 marks a user SID (system SIDs are excluded).

detectors.name: unc-host
detectors.description: Hostname in a UNC path \\HOST\share (device paths \\?\ and \\.\ are excluded by the leading-letter rule).
